text.title: Dep Bump Batching Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Dep Bump Batching Strategy methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Patch bumps batch together; minor bumps batch per ecosystem; major bumps ship as their own PR. Mixing tiers in one PR is forbidden.
rule.rationale: major bumps carry breaking-change review burden that patch tiers do not.

rule.statement: Every batched bump row MUST link to the upstream CHANGELOG/release-notes entry for the exact version delta. 'See upstream' without a URL is rejected.
rule.rationale: review without changelog is not review.

rule.statement: Each bump row records a blast_radius score in {low, medium, high} based on import-graph reach. Rows with score=high cannot share a PR with score=low rows.
rule.rationale: co-batching a risky bump with trivial ones hides the risk behind a green CI run.

rule.statement: Each batch declares the CI job set that must pass before merge (unit, integration, e2e). Bumps with no test coverage MUST be flagged `untested=true`.
Every batch ships with a one-line rollback recipe (revert SHA, lockfile snapshot, or pin downgrade). 'Revert and pray' is rejected.
rule.rationale: rollback rehearsal halves real incident time.

rule.statement: Each batch carries a single named owner. Generic owners (team, devs, us) are rejected.
rule.rationale: Un-owned PRs metastasize in review queues.

rule.statement: When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Dep Bump Batching Strategy under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
